title: Using an array in a function called from a parameter

features.name: parameter initialized via function call returning unpacked array
features.description: Code should include a module-level parameter whose value is initialized by calling a function that returns an unpacked array typedef, triggering constant-function evaluation in the elaboration phase

features.name: function body accessing module parameter array via index
features.description: Code should include a function that reads a module-level unpacked array parameter element by index inside a for loop, where that function is later invoked in a constant context

features.name: bit-select assignment on function return array element
features.description: Code should include an assignment to a specific bit range of an indexed element of a function's return variable, where the function return type is an unpacked integer array

features.name: unpacked array parameter with positional array literal
features.description: Code should include a module-level parameter declared as an unpacked integer array and initialized with a positional array literal using the '{...} syntax

features.name: typedef for unpacked array used as function return type
features.description: Code should include a typedef that defines an unpacked array of integers and is then used as the return type of a function, with the function name used as an implicit return variable inside the body
